rxn_class_summary: Organoelectrophotocatalytic tandem radical cyclization of N-arylacrylamides with formamides or aldehydes to give acylated 3-CF3-2-oxindoles via carbamoyl/acyl radicals generated by phenanthrenequinone (PQ) under combined electrolysis and visible light
electrochemical_role: PQ acts as a photoexcited HAT catalyst that abstracts the formyl C-H of formamide/aldehyde to give a carbamoyl/acyl radical and PQH•; PQH• is reoxidized at the RVC anode (via PQH2) to regenerate PQ, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical 47 after addition/cyclization onto N-arylacrylamide arene

electron_flow: Visible light excites PQ to PQ*, which abstracts the formyl H of formamide/aldehyde (2) to give an acyl/carbamoyl radical (45) and PQH•; 45 adds to the acrylamide 1 and the resulting C-radical cyclizes onto the arene to give cyclohexadienyl radical 47, which is rearomatized either by HAT to PQH• (path A) or by anodic oxidation/deprotonation (path B) to product 3; PQH2 is reoxidized at the RVC anode to close the PQ cycle while H+ is reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.
rate_determining_step: HAT from the formyl C-H of formamide/aldehyde by PQ* to generate the acyl/carbamoyl radical

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: TEMPO-carbamoyl adduct 46 detected by HRMS, supporting a free carbamoyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: control without PQ
probe_reagents.observation: No product 3 formed (nd), PQ is essential

probe_reagents.name: control without electricity
probe_reagents.observation: Yield drops to 12%, anodic turnover of PQ is needed

probe_reagents.name: control without light
probe_reagents.observation: No product (nd); photoexcitation of PQ is required

probe_reagents.name: control without CH3SO3H
probe_reagents.observation: Yield drops to 23%, acid promotes the reaction

probe_reagents.name: reaction under air
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product, radical chain sensitive to O2

probe_reagents.name: alternative catalysts (anthraquinone, naphthalene-1,2- and 1,4-dione)
probe_reagents.observation: Much lower yields (trace, 17%, 26%), highlighting PQ's unique photoelectrocatalytic activity

probe_reagents.name: DMF as formamide substrate
probe_reagents.observation: Inefficient, attributed to competing HAT from N-methyl C-H

notes: Two pathways are proposed for rearomatization of cyclohexadienyl radical 47: path A (HAT to PQH•) and path B (anodic oxidation followed by deprotonation); the authors do not distinguish between them. A single-crystal X-ray structure (CCDC 2270962) of product 3 confirms regiochemistry. PQ plays a dual role as photo-HAT catalyst and anodically recyclable mediator, enabling oxidant- and metal-free conditions with H2 as the cathodic byproduct.
